item.title: Images of Note: I'll have to hand you a lemon.
item.description:
Yeah
Correct, fuck the British Empire.
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA
nyoom
This is what all our memes will look like in 100 years.
owo
AAAAAAAAAAA
07/10/2025, 13:32:07 UTC.
They clearly had cocaine in coke back then too.
slay
If you are on Windows and type ctrl+win+alt+shift+L it takes you to fucking LinkedIn. They have a fucking shortcut for LinkedIn.
B O A T
Listen, The UK's politics is beyond irredeemable and I pray for Scottish Independence, but our plugs are the best designed out there. They're designed with Child Safety in mind and they allow us to wire them ourselves and install fuses. Can you say the same, America?
Judging by who's chasing who, I think that other cat didn't belong in that house.
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

Suggested Track Playlists: (HIT SHUFFLE) YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLoIzKPbKfwniKOaadw-tB8kaEQiUQ1Yp4 Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/playlist/6cqV52puia81vNnPHxcz1W?si=yoUknxpQQVql9E-NZRQ4 *TOGGLE MATURE CONTENT IN SETTINGS TO VIEW OUR POSTS* ICM SQUAD: @vazquezdaniel, @ohvalleyofplenty, @nichoDriver, @Dirgekins, @onewallpaperaweek, @thedownlowe, @TheForbiddenSemicolon, @bagoffishstix, @JayKillBam, @majkongskull, @DingoJ0nes, @HandsomePenguin, @MrJwinkyface, @Gorgoror, @ciadmillefailte, @kaeldra, @DerpyVaz ICM TIP JAR: https://www.patreon.com/InterwebChexMix Just to let you know, we do not take credit for any random art, comics, or photos featured in this collection; we only take credit for the daily effort of compiling the collections themselves. Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos
item.dc:creator: MetaPathos

item.title: "Good Morning Grandkids"
item.description: I'm sad to see her Skyrim content come to an end, for a while there I was looking forward to her videos every day. It's still crazy impressive that she was able to keep it up this long, especially considered she suffered a stroke a couple years ago and had to completely relearn how to play Skyrim, edit videos, and post to YouTube! Edit: Meant to add she's so loved by the Skyrim community a modder added her to Skyrim as a follower! (She even has a cute little cabin.) https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/45956 Shirley will also be featured in the Elder Scrolls VI as an NPC (: https://gamerant.com/the-elder-scrolls-6-skyrim-grandma-npc-importance-follower-quests-story/
item.dc:creator: nomorehate

item.title: I Stole This DUMP, So You Don't Have To. Tuesday Random Meme Dump Of Things That Make me Chuckle IRL
item.description:
1. These dudes are just amazing
2. Sound ON for this game
3. *Leather noises*
4. That is pretty amazing duel
5
6. Plot TWIST
7. True fact: this girl is used to power small cities
8. Just a velo baby
9. A-hole cat
10. My trying to navigate social occasions
11
12
13. F around and find out
14. Squire attack
15. Thanks for checking out the dump, finally I want to share something from the game I'm working on. It's a wacky Stanley Parable-inspired thing, that hopefully will resonate with people with weird humor. What do you think of this particular area of the game? Do you think the summations are funny or I should try and improved them. Oh, and if it's not to much hassle, please give the game a wishlist, it makes a world of difference to small dev teams like me and my brother: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: I'm getting older, having a not so good day, watching these 80s cartoon intro themes made me feel better!
item.description:
#1 The Transformers. They were the biggest part of my childhood, so I had to start us off with this one! Anyone seen the newest movie Transformers One yet?
#2 Challenge of the Gobots. I had to follow Transformers with what was considered the "knock offs." I had some of the Gobots toys too, so these held a special, albeit smaller, place in my heart.
#3 G.I. Joe. Another Hasbro hit. I wasn't as into the G.I. Joe toys, but I watched the cartoons very religiously. I included the Season 2 intro because of Serpentor and Sergeant Slaughter.
#4 Inspector Gadget. I freaking loved Penny's computer watch! Also, side anecdote, I was watching Inspector Gadget when our 26" console TV broke down and Inspector Gadget just vanished from the screen. It was a sad day.
#5 Jem. 9 year old me thought Jem/Jerrica was pretty hot.
#6 M.A.S.K. Like the Transformers and Gobots, I loved how the vehicles transformed. The commercials for the toys were very catchy as well. I never collected any of the toys though.
#7 Voltron: Defender of the Universe. I wanted these toys SO BAD when I was little. Never got them :( But just as well, I probably got a couple extra Transformers toys because I didn't spend any money one the lions. Speaking of Transformers, Peter Cullen (Optimus Prime) narrated the intro.
#8 Sabre Rider and the Star Sherrifs. This has got to be one of the catchier intro themes because I remember the theme more than the cartoon itself. Peter Cullen really made his rounds in the mid 80's, he was the voice of Ramrod, the big transformable ship/robot in the cartoon. Also, April was a cutie.
#9 The Smurfs. I remember having to watch this every Saturday morning. I included the 3rd season's intro because that's the one I remember the most... plus the first season's intro was boring IMO.
#10 Silverhawks. Another memorable intro. Just like Sabre Rider, I remember the intro more than the actual cartoons, but the shiny armor they wore was freaking awesome, and they had a matching space ship to boot... that shot out rainbows!
#11 Jayce and the Wheeled Warriors. I didn't actually watch this cartoon that much, I think it was one of those that followed a cartoon that I watched a lot. But I still remember it well because not only was the intro catchy, but the toys were freaking awesome with the interchangeable parts.
#12 Thundercats. Thunder... Thunder... Thunder... enough said. Oh, and Cheetarah...
#13 Duck Tales. This was something I had to watch after school everyday. Disney did really well with this one. Also, the video game for it on the NES was pretty good too.
#14 Chip 'm Dale Rescue Rangers. Disney was on a roll, this was another one I had to watch after school. This was one of my wife's favorite cartoons too.
#15 Turbo Teen. Now that I look back, this is kind of a WTF? But my love for cars captivated me... not gonna lie, I wanted to transform into a car too!
#16 The Mighty Orbots. Yup, a total Voltron knock off, and it showed because it was cancelled in less than one season. I loved it though, and loved the theme song.
#17 Pac Man. I loved video games and this was one of the, if not, first video game cartoons I ever watched.
#18 These next 3 are from the Sunday morning "Comic Strip" group of cartoons. First up are the TigerSharks, clearly a knock off of Thundercats but in the water. I still loved it though because I thought it was awesome to be able to transform into super hero water creatures. That was something Thundercats didn't do.
#19 Karate Kat. Yeah he was cool, and I definitely agreed with that sentiment, so I watched it religiously.
#20 Street Frogs. You know, this might have sparked my love for R&B and Hip Hop in my teenage years. Actually no... rapping frogs are just awesome!
#21 Heathcliff. I'm really not sure why I loved this cartoon so much... I guess Heathcliff was a small, weak looking cat who ended up being a bad ass that always came out on top.
#22 Flintstone Kids. I watched The Flintstones a lot when I was little, but it wasn't from the 80s, so I added the Flintstone Kids to this list instead. Yeah, it was a total unoriginal cash grab, but it was a really fun cartoon. I know there are lots of cartoons I'm missing, but these were some of the ones that immediately came to mind. Feel free to add more in the comments!
item.dc:creator: pdp1
